name: Oracle Health (Cerner) Millennium FHIR API
what_it_does: Oracle Health (Cerner) Millennium FHIR R4 API for electronic health records and clinical data interoperability. Enables AI agents to manage patient demographic and clinical data access via FHIR standard, handle clinical documentation and note retrieval for care coordination, access medication, allergy, and order management data, retrieve lab result and diagnostic imaging data for clinical support, manage appointment and scheduling workflow automation, handle clinical decision support data integration, access population health management and cohort analytics, retrieve quality measure and regulatory reporting data, manage care plan and care team communication workflows, and integrate Cerner clinical data with HIE, analytics, and care management platforms.
use_cases: Retrieving patient clinical records from care coordination agents
Accessing medication and order data from pharmacy automation agents
Managing clinical documentation from care management agents
Integrating Cerner data with analytics from population health agents
not_for: Consumer health apps without Oracle Health customer relationship
General document management without clinical EHR workflow integration
best_when: A healthcare organization using Oracle Health (Cerner) Millennium wants AI agents to automate FHIR-based clinical data access, care coordination, population analytics, quality reporting, and HIE integration.
avoid_when: CRITICAL HIPAA RISK: All Cerner API access to PHI requires HIPAA BAA and minimum necessary data compliance. Automated clinical decision support must follow FDA guidance for clinical decision support software. Oracle Health code app approval required for third-party applications accessing production patient data.

auth.notes: Oracle Health uses SMART on FHIR OAuth 2.0. Backend service applications use client credentials with JWT. Patient-facing apps use authorization code with EHR launch context. Oracle Health code platform for approved app integrations. No native webhooks — polling or HL7 messaging for event integration. HIPAA BAA required for PHI access. Cerner Open Developer Experience (code) program.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Cerner sandbox environment free for development; production requires Oracle Health customer relationship
pricing.paid_tiers: Oracle Health software licensing by healthcare organization; no separate FHIR API fees for approved integrations
pricing.notes: Kansas City, Missouri. Oracle Health (formerly Cerner). Acquired by Oracle Corporation (2022, $28.3B). EHR market #2 after Epic. 25%+ US hospital market share. Millennium EHR platform. Oracle Health Code program for approved integrations. Strong community hospitals and government health systems. Competes with Epic for EHR market.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits per OAuth application; varies by Oracle Health customer deployment

known_agent_gotchas: CRITICAL HIPAA RISK: PHI access requires HIPAA BAA — all AI agent access to patient data must be logged for HIPAA audit trail requirements
Oracle acquisition integration — Cerner is now Oracle Health; product and API roadmap evolving post-acquisition; verify current API surface
SMART on FHIR scoping — scope at minimum necessary FHIR resources for HIPAA compliance; avoid broad scopes in automated workflows
Oracle Health code approval — production access requires Oracle Health code program approval; sandbox available for development
Per-customer deployment — each Cerner customer has unique instance; API endpoint varies by organization; no single Oracle Health API gateway
ONC HTI-1 compliance — new ONC requirements for API access (21st Century Cures Act); verify compliance requirements for US healthcare integration
agent_readiness.security_notes: EHR and clinical data. HIPAA, SOC2, HL7-FHIR. SMART on FHIR/OAuth2. US. Patient PHI and clinical data.

security_score_components.security_notes: EHR. HIPAA, SOC2. SMART on FHIR/OAuth2. US. Patient PHI and clinical data.
